Oren P. Noah, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Sandra Phillips, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT JOHN CRANE, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant John Crane, Inc.'s demurrer to plaintiffs' first amended complaint is overruled. Plaintiffs' complaint contains sufficient allegations that Mr. Phillips died from an asbestos-related illness. The absence of any allegation as to date of decedent's death does not render the first amended complaint deficient as a matter of law. Defendant has 20 days to respond to the first amended complaint. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-22-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Kurt Putnam, Esq., of HASSARD BONNINGTON (415-288-9800), appearing for defendant / moving party John Crane, Inc.
Lance R. Stewart, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Sandra Phillips, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT JOHN CRANE, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Argued and tentative ruling vacated. The Court issued its ruling as follows: Defendant John Crane Inc.'s general demurrer to plaintiffs' complaint is sustained as to the issue whether Merle Phillips died from an asbestos-related illness with 10 dyas leave to amend. The Court does not take judicial notice of the death certificate. Even if the court were to take judicial notice of the existence of the death certificate as part of the records of the court (E.C. 452(d)), the Court does not take judicial notice of the truth of the facts stated in the death certificate itself for purposes of ruling on this demurrer. As to the second ground for the general demurrer, it is sustained with 10 days leave to amend to give plaintiffs an opportunity, if they are able to do so, to allege facts sufficient to meet the requirements of Bockrath. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Reporter: Loretta Najera, CSR #5153 = (220/MJM)
